properties.remark: FS RD 674 CLOSED EAST OF MCCALL DUE TO MUDSLIDE.
SMALL BRANCHES DOWN. BLOWING DUST.
ONE TREE BLOWN DOWN AND TIN ROOFING REMOVED FROM THE ROOF OF A BARN IN JEROME. APPARENT MICROBURST AS NO SURROUNDING OBS REPORTED WINDS ABOVE 20 MPH. IMAGES FORWARDED BY KMVT.
SPOTTER 10 WEST OF DIAMOND REPORTED QUARTER-SIZED HAIL AND 30 MPH WINDS.
CANYON COUNTY DEPUTY REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL NEAR IMMIGRANT ROAD AND CANYON CREEK ROAD.
ELMORE COUNTY DEPUTY REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL NEAR IMMIGRANT ROAD AND CANYON CREEK ROAD.
A FEW FOREST SERVICE ROADS WERE WASHED OUT IN THE BEAVER CREEK BURN SCAR.

FLASH FLOODING AND LAND SLIDES ON THE SOUTH END OF THE ELK COMPLEX BURN SCAR NEAR ANDERSON RANCH DAM. SEVERAL ROADS COMPROMISED INCLUDING ANDERSON RANCH DAM ROAD AND HIGHWAY 20 WHICH HAVE BEEN CLOSED.
HEAVY RAINS IN THE PRAIRIE ARE HAVE CAUSED LANDSLIDES ON THE PRAIRIE RD LEADING TO COW CREEK.
THE BLACKS CREEK RD, FROM THE Y STOP TO THE ADA COUNTY LINE, HAS BEEN CLOSED DUE TO RECENT FLOODING UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTED POWER LINES DOWN BETWEEN HAGERMAN AND BLISS.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTED POWER OUTAGES IN GOODING.
THE FMO FROM THE FAIRFIELD RANGER STATION REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND O.58 INCHES OF RAIN.
POWER POLES DOWN 2 MILES NW OF HAGERMAN.
HANSEN BRIDGE
TRAIL GULCH RAWS
NEWS MEDIA REPORTS TREES DOWN ON EAST-RIDGE ROAD IN KIMBERLY
IDARNG3D2 RAWS
FLASH FLOOD ON MCMULLEN CREEK WASHING OUT ROADS NEAR INTERSECTION OF E-2900-N ROAD AND N-3500-E
DEBRIS FLOW ACROSS HIGHWAY 21 NEAR HAYFORK CAMPGROUND
OVER 100 TREES DOWN ON THE WARREN TO BURGDORF ROAD FROM THUNDERSTORMS. REPORTED BY PAYETTE NF DISPATCH. TIME APPROXIMATED FROM LIGHTNING DATA.
KNVT-11 METEOROLOGIST REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL WITH ISOLATED GOLF-BALL SIZED HAIL. THERE IS ALSO FLOODING ASSOCIATED WITH THIS EVENT.
SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER-SIZED HAIL AND SOME FLOODING.
TWIN FALLS EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED GOLF-BALL SIZED HAIL.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A LOT OF QUARTER-SIZED HAIL.
SPOTTER REPORTED 1 INCH HAIL, WATER RUNNING THROUGH HIS SHOP, AND DOWNED BRANCHES IN HIS YARD MEASURING 2.5 INCHES.
SPOTTER REPORTED A LOT OF 1 INCH HAIL, A TORN UP FRONT PORCH, AND THE NEIGHBORS ROOF PULLED BACK.
SPOTTER REPORTED PING PONG BALL SIZED HAIL. HE SAID THE GROUND IS ALMOST WHITE AND THAT HIS CORN SUFFERED SOME DAMAGE.
LARGE AMOUNTS OF LARGE HAIL IN TWIN FALLS. THIS COMBINED WITH HEAVY RAIN TO PRODUCE MINOR STREET FLOODING AS WELL.
